market_context: US stock futures for Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq are sliding significantly due to escalating US-Iran conflict, with Dow futures down ~800 points on Tuesday and continued gap down today amid rising oil prices (Brent up ~8%). VIX futures indicate heightened volatility, up over 9% in 5-day period with stochastic at extreme levels. Geopolitical tensions are driving flight to safety in dollar and gold, pressuring risk assets while boosting oil and defense sectors.[1][2][5][6]

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are down amid Iran war escalation, following Tuesday's 800-point Dow drop and ongoing gap down; oil surge to 8% adds pressure on equities.[1][6] VIX up 9.15% signals sustained volatility, with futures off lows but still negative ~1.1%.[5][6] Pre-market points to risk-off sentiment persisting through session close.

Nasdaq futures gapping down on oil rebound and geopolitical volatility, with NDX eyeing 24,770 pivot amid broader market slide.[2] Conflict-driven sell-off hits tech-heavy index harder, as seen in prior session retreats.[1] Heightened fear indicators support continued downside.

Oil prices spiking 8% on Brent/WTI (from 13% intraday high) due to US-Iran strikes entering third day, with spot LNG potentially up 30%.[6] Geopolitical escalation in Middle East directly boosts crude demand fears and supply risks.[1][6] Futures orderly but elevated, favoring energy ETF gains.

Gold up over 2% as safe-haven amid futures slide and dollar strength from risk-off flows.[6] Iran conflict volatility drives investors to metals, aligning with VIX surge and equity retreat.[1][5] Expect modest further gains if tensions persist.
